description_long: This house features a traditional timber-framed exterior with a white infill and dark wooden beams arranged in a decorative pattern. The structure is three stories tall, with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. Windows are arranged in symmetrical vertical rows, each with a grid of small panes, and the main entryway is a simple dark door at ground level. The side of the house reveals an attached section with a lower roof, also timber-framed, suggesting an extension or annex. The rear and side facades maintain the same timber-and-plaster motif, with no visible ornamentation or porches. The overall appearance is reminiscent of medieval or Tudor-style urban dwellings commonly found in historic European towns.
description: A three-story, timber-framed house with a steep gable roof, symmetrical windows, and a simple entryway, evoking a historic European style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with three stories, symmetrical windows, and the main entry door at the bottom.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full height of the house, the steep gable roof, and the attached lower annex section.
description_view_2: Angled view showing both the main building and the annex, with visible window arrangements and rooflines.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the plain timber-framed wall, the gable roof, and the annex extending from the side.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple single door at ground level, no porch or decorative elements, flush with facade
